turns.speaker: host
turns.text: Today we discuss the emergence of large language models with strong reasoning and instruction-following capabilities, giving rise to new classes of software systems that collaborate in complex tasks. The simplest form of agentic architecture is the single-agent with tool use pattern. In this pattern, a single LLM operates in a loop of reasoning and acting: it receives a user query, reasons about what action to take, calls an external tool, observes the result, and continues reasoning until it arrives at a final answer.
